title: [SDB] Implement SRDB CSV import/export for engineering workflows
body:
## Context
As the SRDB grows to hundreds of parameters across many subsystems, YAML files become difficult to maintain. Engineers typically work in Excel or tab-separated ASCII tools.

## Objective
Provide CSV import/export tools so engineers can maintain parameters in familiar tools while the YAML remains the authoritative source.

## Design
- tools/srdb_to_csv.py: export all SRDB baselines to a single CSV file
- tools/csv_to_srdb.py: import CSV back to domain YAML files
- CSV schema: canonical_name, description, unit, dtype, classification, domain, model_id, valid_range_min, valid_range_max, pus_apid, pus_service, pus_subservice, pus_parameter_id
- Excel opens CSV natively — no special tooling required

## Future
- XTCE export (SVF-DEV-097) for YAMCS interoperability — M10
- MIB import (SVF-DEV-098) for legacy ground segment migration — M10
- Dedicated SRDB editor tool (long term)

## Implements
SVF-DEV-096, SVF-DEV-097, SVF-DEV-098 (partial)
